steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: Sentence 2 says NASA intentionally crashed a probe into Dimorphos. Sentence 3 says Dimorphos’s orbital period was permanently altered. The second event is the direct result of the first — the blank needs a cause-and-effect transition.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In addition,’: Signals an extra, independent fact — but the orbital change is not independent; it is the direct result of the crash. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Specifically,’: Signals a narrower detail within the same point — but the orbital change is a consequence, not a specification of the crash itself. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Consequently,’: Signals that what follows is a direct result of what preceded it — exactly the cause-and-effect relationship here. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘In comparison,’: Signals a contrast between two things being measured against each other — no comparison is being made in these sentences. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
